definition: A taste as the object of the tongue within the six sense fields.
notes: The project prefers taste because it is the cleanest analytical rendering in ordinary English. Flavor is acceptable when the sentence benefits from a more concrete or sensuous register, but essence should be avoided because it belongs to a different semantic track altogether.

context_rules.context: In most analytical and source-facing contexts
In lighter prose where the concrete gustatory quality is being emphasized

example_phrases.notes: Object-side term in standard sense-base formulas.
